periods.title: Key West waterfront and city grid in 1889
periods.summary: The first online Sanborn edition for Key West contains 16 sheets. Its graphic-index sheet records the compact pre-rail city, waterfront blocks, wharves, and the undeveloped northern edge before Trumbo Point was created.
highlights: The Library of Congress dates this edition to April 1889 and provides all 16 sheets in the public domain.
The index map is useful for comparing the pre-rail shoreline, street grid, wharves, and salt-pond margins.
This initial rectangular placement is adjustable and should not be used for parcel or shoreline measurement.

overlay.title: 1889 Key West Sanborn graphic index — experimental fit
overlay.attribution: Sanborn Map Company / Library of Congress, April 1889; public domain

periods.title: Key West urban and waterfront expansion in 1892
periods.summary: The April 1892 Sanborn edition expands to 23 sheets and provides a second close-spaced record of the city and working waterfront before the Overseas Railway project.
highlights: The 1892 edition can be compared directly with 1889 to identify new blocks, industrial uses, and waterfront structures.
The source is a public-domain Library of Congress scan hosted by Wikimedia Commons.
Manual alignment remains enabled because the index sheet is not a modern orthographic GIS layer.

overlay.title: 1892 Key West Sanborn map — experimental fit
overlay.attribution: Sanborn Map Company / Library of Congress, April 1892; public domain

periods.title: Key West immediately before the railway era
periods.summary: The April 1899 Sanborn edition contains 24 sheets and offers the clearest late-nineteenth-century city index currently available online. It predates the Overseas Extension and the Trumbo terminal landfill.
highlights: The graphic index depicts the developed city, numbered sheet coverage, waterfront edges, and major institutional areas.
This is a key baseline for separating nineteenth-century urban growth from twentieth-century rail and naval reclamation.
The full 24-sheet edition remains linked in the source catalog for block-level research.

overlay.title: 1899 Key West Sanborn graphic index — experimental fit
overlay.attribution: Sanborn Map Company / Library of Congress, April 1899; public domain

periods.title: Key West in the first railway year
periods.summary: The April 1912 Sanborn edition contains 33 sheets and was produced only months after the first train reached Key West. It is the most important urban-map addition for examining the new Trumbo terminal district and its relationship to the older city.
highlights: The Library of Congress dates the edition to April 1912, shortly after the January railway arrival.
Its expanded 33-sheet coverage can support detailed follow-on work around Trumbo Point, depots, wharves, and industrial parcels.
The currently displayed overview is an adjustable visual fit; individual waterfront sheets should later be mosaicked and controlled separately.
